mainEntity.name: How much does a Mercedes SL500 / SL550 / SL560 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned SL V8 engine typically costs £2,500–£6,500 supply only depending on engine generation (M117, M119, M113, M273 or M278). On average, a full engine replacement can run anywhere between £3,000 and £8,500 depending on the car model and engine type. Fitting adds £1,200–£2,000. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your vehicle.

mainEntity.name: Is the Mercedes SL500 / SL550 / SL560 engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends on which generation your SL has. The M117 in the R107 is a legendary engine with near‑mythical durability – engines exceeding 250,000 miles are not unusual. The M119 in the early R129 is a masterpiece but has distributor and wiring vulnerabilities. The M113 in the late R129 and early R230 is robust but suffers from sludge and carbon build‑up. The M273 in the facelift R230 has a well‑documented timing chain idler sprocket weakness. The M278 in the R231 is a sophisticated twin‑turbo V8 with oil leak and turbo concerns. Regular oil changes (every 8,000‑10,000 miles) are essential for all these engines.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Mercedes SL500 / SL550 / SL560?

acceptedAnswer.text: On the R107 (M117), the most common issue is timing chain guide wear. On the R129 early (M119), distributor caps and wiring harnesses are the main weaknesses. On the R129 late and R230 early (M113), sludge and carbon build‑up from short trips are the main concerns. On the R230 facelift (M273), the defining weakness is timing chain idler sprocket failure. On the R231 (M278), oil leaks and turbocharger issues are the primary concerns.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing an SL V8 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and condition. Against a tidy £5,000–£20,000 SL, a £2,500–£6,500 recon is 15‑60% of the car's value – often worth it on well‑maintained examples. If the M273 timing chain idler has failed or the engine has suffered catastrophic failure, a full engine replacement may be necessary. Get a full diagnostic assessment before committing. The SL is a modern classic, so well‑preserved examples may justify the investment.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my SL's engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The R107 560SL uses the M117.968. The R129 (1990‑1998) SL500 uses the M119.972. The R129 (1998‑2001) and R230 (2001‑2006) SL500 use the M113.963. The R230 facelift (2006‑2011) SL500/SL550 uses the M273.965. The R231 (2012‑2020) SL550 uses the M278.928. The engine code is stamped on the engine block and listed on your V5C. For the M273, check the engine serial number – later engines (above approximately 80,000) are generally safe from the idler sprocket issue.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair an SL V8 engine issue, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most issues – M117 timing chain guides (if caught early), M119 distributor/wiring issues, M113 sludge, M273 oil leaks and timing chain idler wear (if caught early), M278 oil leaks – are repairable. However, if the M273 idler sprocket has failed or the timing chain has snapped on any engine, a full engine replacement is usually more cost‑effective than a rebuild.

eraMap.sourceNote: This table is the single source every other section on this page must match. Sections 2, 4, and 6 reference it rather than restating conflicting figures.

replacementCosts.h2: Mercedes SL500 / SL550 / SL560 Engine Replacement Costs

rows.bestFor: M117/M119/M113 no longer available new; M273/M278 limited availability

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the SL V8 pricing across generations. A reconditioned SL V8 engine typically costs £2,500–£4,800 supply only depending on engine generation. On average, a full engine replacement can run anywhere between £3,000 and £8,500 depending on the car model and engine type. The M278 is at the upper end of the range. Mercedes-Benz SL replacement engines are available through specialist suppliers with 12‑month warranties.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an SL V8 engine typically takes 12‑18 hours at £75‑£110/hr (independent Mercedes specialist). The SL's low‑slung bonnet and complex engine bay packaging add significant labour complexity, especially for the R230 with its retractable hardtop and ABC suspension.

cta.label: → Compare SL V8 engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: M113 Sludge & Carbon Build‑up (R129/R230 SL500, 1998–2006)
problems.symptoms: Increased oil consumption, blow‑by, rough idle, tapping noise on start‑up, oil sludge contamination. Carbon build‑up around piston oil control rings in high‑mileage M113 engines is the main reason for increased blow‑by, oil consumption, and oil sludge.
problems.repairCost: £800–£2,500 [PLACEHOLDER] (engine flush, oil control ring cleaning, or cylinder head overhaul).
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (if caught early; ignored sludge can cause catastrophic engine failure).

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if you notice tapping noises, oil consumption, or sludge.

problems.recommendation: The M113 engine doesn't like short trips — it needs to get hot to burn off moisture and prevent sludge. A gooey black sludge can build up on internal parts and oil ways. Using the wrong oil or deferred maintenance will accelerate this issue. Regular oil changes (every 8,000‑10,000 miles) using the correct MB‑spec oil are essential. If you hear tapping on start‑up, have the engine inspected immediately.

problems.title: M119 Distributor Cap & Wiring Harness Issues (R129 SL500, 1990–1998)
problems.symptoms: Rough idle, misfiring, surging, stalling at idle, hesitation on acceleration, engine dying when coming to a stop. The distributor caps and ignition leads are the Achilles heel of the M119.
problems.repairCost: £400–£1,200 [PLACEHOLDER] (distributor cap and rotor replacement, ignition leads, wiring harness inspection).

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if rough running or stalling occurs.

problems.recommendation: The M119's distributor caps are a known weak point. Moisture and dirt can cause misfires and stalling. The lower and upper engine wiring harnesses can also degrade — if they are still the originals, they will eventually fail. If you experience rough running or stalling, have the ignition system and wiring harnesses inspected.

problems.title: M273 Timing Chain Idler Sprocket Failure (R230 SL500/SL550, 2006–2011)
problems.symptoms: Engine warning light with timing codes, rattling noise from the front of the engine, rough running, loss of power, potential catastrophic engine failure. The M273 V8 is susceptible to timing chain idler sprocket failure (often referred to as "balance shaft" failure).
problems.repairCost: £2,000–£4,000 [PLACEHOLDER] (timing chain idler sprocket and timing components replacement — engine‑out job).

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if the engine warning light appears with timing codes or you hear rattling, book an inspection immediately.

problems.recommendation: The M273's timing chain idler sprocket is the defining weakness of this engine. Mercedes manufactured the idler gear with softer metal than required, leading to premature wear. The M272 V6 and M273 V8 both utilise an idler gear that powers camshaft timing. Engines with serial numbers in certain ranges are affected. Check your engine serial number — later engines (serial numbers above approximately 80,000) are generally safe. If you see timing codes or hear rattling, have the timing system inspected immediately.

problems.title: M273 Oil Separator & Cam Plug Leaks (R230 SL500/SL550, 2006–2011)
problems.symptoms: Oil spots under the vehicle, burning oil smell, low oil level warning, visible oil leak from the rear of the engine. The oil separator is a common area for oil leaks on the M273 engine.

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if oil leak is detected.

problems.recommendation: The M273's oil separator and cam plugs are known leak points. If you notice oil spots or a burning smell, have the oil separator and cam plugs inspected. Replacement is relatively straightforward and much cheaper than letting the leak worsen.

problems.title: M117 Timing Chain Guides (R107 560SL, 1986–1989)
problems.symptoms: Rattling noise from the front of the engine on cold start, metallic ticking, engine warning light. The timing chain guides are made of plastic that can become brittle and break with age.
problems.repairCost: £800–£2,000 [PLACEHOLDER] (timing chain guides, tensioner, and chain replacement).
problems.replacementCost: £2,500–£4,500 [PLACEHOLDER] (if chain snaps and damages the engine).

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattling, book an inspection immediately.

problems.recommendation: The M117's timing chain guides are a known wear item. While the M117 is a robust engine capable of high mileage, the plastic guides can become brittle with age. If the chain breaks, it can cause catastrophic engine damage. A preventative chain and guide replacement is far cheaper than a full engine replacement. Regular oil changes are essential to prolong chain life. As one owner noted: "they all need prompt chain and guide maintenance, frequent oil changes, and hate being overheated".

problems.title: M278 Oil Leaks & Turbocharger Issues (R231 SL550, 2012–2020)
problems.symptoms: Oil spots under the vehicle, burning oil smell, loss of power, turbo lag, whistling or grinding noise from the engine bay. The M278 is known for oil leaks from the oil cooler and turbocharger oil feed lines.
problems.repairCost: £800–£2,500 [PLACEHOLDER] (oil cooler seal replacement, turbo oil line replacement).

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if oil leak is detected or power loss occurs.

problems.recommendation: The M278 4.7L bi‑turbo V8 is a sophisticated engine, but the turbocharger oil feed lines and oil cooler seals can develop leaks. If you notice oil spots or a burning smell, have the engine inspected immediately. Regular oil changes using the correct MB‑spec oil are essential to prolong turbo life.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Mercedes SL V8 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Worth fixing — common on high‑mileage M113s
Worth fixing — common and well‑understood repair

rows.problem: M273 timing chain idler sprocket wear

Full engine replacement often more cost‑effective
Catch it early — cheaper than engine replacement
Full engine replacement — valvetrain damage is likely
Full engine replacement often more cost‑effective

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Mercedes SL V8? Check These First
buyIf: * Full Mercedes service history with regular oil changes (every 8,000‑10,000 miles)
* R107 (M117): Timing chain guides replaced — this is critical on higher‑mileage cars
* R129 early (M119): Distributor caps and wiring harnesses replaced
* R129 late / R230 early (M113): No sludge, regular oil changes, good maintenance history
* R230 facelift (M273): Engine serial number above 80,000 — idler sprocket issue resolved
* R231 (M278): No oil leaks, turbo system in good health
avoidIf: * R107 (M117): Any sign of timing chain rattle on cold start — guide warning
* R129 early (M119): Any sign of rough idle or stalling — distributor or wiring warning
* R129 late / R230 early (M113): Any sign of sludge or tapping — oil system warning
* R230 facelift (M273): Any sign of rattling or timing codes — idler sprocket warning
* R231 (M278): Any sign of oil leaks — turbo or oil cooler warning
* Poor service history — these engines are intolerant of neglect

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The Mercedes SL500, SL550 and SL560 represent some of the most iconic V8‑powered roadsters ever built. The M117 in the R107 560SL is a legendary engine with near‑mythical durability. The M119 in the early R129 is a DOHC masterpiece with a sensitive ignition system. The M113 in the late R129 and early R230 is a robust SOHC V8 that doesn't like short trips. The M273 in the facelift R230 is a powerful DOHC V8 with a well‑documented timing chain idler sprocket vulnerability. The M278 in the R231 is a sophisticated twin‑turbo V8 with oil leak and turbo concerns. Buy on service history and engine serial number above all else — a well‑maintained SL V8 is a rewarding and collectable car; a neglected example can be expensive to repair. As one specialist noted, the M113 "doesn't like short journeys" and "no amount of oil changes will stave off piston ring damage and cylinder wall scoring".

cta.label: → Compare SL V8 engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Mercedes SL V8 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: 1. Cold Start — Any rattling? (M117/M273 timing chain warning — stop immediately)
2. Service History — Regular oil changes (every 8,000‑10,000 miles) — this is non‑negotiable
3. Engine Serial Number — M273: Check if above 80,000 (idler sprocket issue resolved)
4. Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any timing codes? (M273 warning)
5. Oil Leaks — Check under the vehicle and around the engine for any signs of leakage
6. Running Quality — Any rough idle, stalling, or hesitation? (M119 distributor or M113 sludge warning)
7. Oil Consumption — Does it use more than 1L per 1,000 miles? (M113 sludge warning)
8. MOT History — Check for emissions‑related failures or any advisory notes

engineCodes.matchNote: This table's rows match Section 1B's Era Map exactly.

items.value: * Service schema price range: £1,500 (lowest used) to £6,500 (highest rebuilt) — spans full Era Map history

items.value: * Generation Pages needed: Mercedes SL‑Class Generation Pages (for cost inheritance)

items.label: Section 9 (Trust + Final CTA), meta tags, Open Graph, Twitter Card, and the comprehensive JSON-LD schema. They're following the established pattern of a two-prompt production system outlined in the original "IBS Variant Prompt.docx" file.
items.value: My response needs to mirror the structure of the last "Part 2" I generated for the G63. I need to create Section 9 with three trust points, a final CTA, and the correct button text. I need the meta title and description, Open Graph tags, and the Twitter Card. Most importantly, I need to build the JSON-LD schema with the correct blocks: BreadcrumbList, two ItemList instances (one for the Era Map, one for the engine codes), FAQPage, Organization, Service, Dataset, and WebPage. The price range in the Service schema must span the full era map history, from the lowest used price to the highest rebuilt price for the M278. I also need to include the Production Note with all the required checks and confirmations, and list the live verification sources used.

items.label: The challenge is to ensure all the details match the data provided in Part 1. The Era Map has 8 rows across 5 engine codes (M117.968, M119.972, M113.963, M273.965, M278.928), so both ItemLists need to reflect that. The Service schema's lowPrice should be the lowest from the used engine costs in Section 2 (£1,500 for the M117/M113 era), and the highPrice should be the highest from the rebuilt costs for the M278 (£6,500). I also need to maintain the brand voice—for Mercedes, it should be "Refinement & reassurance," so the trust points and final CTA should sound polished, confident, and reassuring, emphasizing the iconic nature of the SL.
